analysis.consistency: Consistent with standard D&D 5e class features regarding ritual casting. Clerics gain this ability, often tied to their class level or preparation mechanics.
analysis.quality: High quality. The description is clear, concise, and accurately reflects the core mechanic: ability to use prepared spells with the ritual tag for ritual casting.

mechanics.trigger: Attempting to cast a spell as a ritual.
requirements: The spell must have the 'ritual' tag.
The spell must be currently prepared by the cleric.
mechanics.effect: Allows the spell to be cast using the ritual casting time/procedure instead of expending a spell slot.

llm_audit.suggestions: None required. The feature is standard and well-defined.
